summary: mongoose-lean-hooks 0.5.5 fetches and executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com on require()
details: index.js appends a hidden IIFE after `module.exports = leanhooksPlugin;`, padded with hundreds of tab characters to keep it off-screen in casual review. When any consumer require()s the package, the IIFE runs `axios.get('https://jsonkeeper.com/b/LZCY3')` and passes the returned `cookie` field to `new Function('require',...)`, then invokes the constructed function with the host's `require`. This gives the operator arbitrary code execution in the consumer's Node process with access to `require` (filesystem, network, environment variables). The advertised purpose is a small Mongoose timestamps plugin, but 0.5.5 also adds runtime dependencies (axios, sqlite3, request, defaults) that the plugin logic does not use — axios is present specifically to power the remote-exec loader. The payload host (jsonkeeper.com) is an anonymous, author-mutable paste service, so today's fetched code can be swapped for anything at any time without a package republish. Behavior shape is consistent with a hijack/malicious republish of a legacy package name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
